# Feature Specification: Double-Sided Face Rendering

**Feature Branch**: `[main]`  
**Created**: 2026-04-27  
**Status**: Draft  
**Input**: User description: "there is a bug where face is generated by extrude but not rendered because it's normal is pointing in wrong direction; make faces render on both sides"

## User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*

### User Story 1 - See every extruded face (Priority: P1)

As a modeler, I want every face created by an extrusion to stay visible even if its orientation is reversed so the solid I just made always appears complete in the viewport.

**Why this priority**: A missing rendered face makes the resulting solid look broken and prevents users from trusting what they just created.

**Independent Test**: Create an extrusion that previously produced a missing face because of reversed orientation. Confirm that the same face remains visible while viewing it from either side.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an extrusion creates a face whose orientation points away from the viewer, **When** the body is shown in the viewport, **Then** that face is still rendered instead of disappearing.
2. **Given** an extruded face is visible from the front, **When** the user orbits to the opposite side of that same face, **Then** the face remains visible from the back as well.

---

### User Story 2 - Inspect completed geometry from any angle (Priority: P1)

As a designer, I want to rotate around an extruded body and keep seeing complete exterior surfaces so I can inspect the shape without gaps appearing as the camera moves.

**Why this priority**: Viewing the model from multiple angles is a core CAD workflow, and disappearing faces interrupt review, editing, and confidence in the result.

**Independent Test**: Extrude a profile, orbit the camera around the resulting body, and verify that no outward-facing extruded surface disappears solely because the face orientation is reversed.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an extruded body contains a mix of normally oriented and reversed faces, **When** the user rotates around the model, **Then** all outward-facing extruded surfaces remain visible throughout the orbit.
2. **Given** the user is reviewing the result of an extrusion, **When** they move the camera to inspect a previously missing face, **Then** the surface is rendered consistently enough to confirm the geometry is present.

---

### User Story 3 - Preserve normal rendering behavior for unaffected faces (Priority: P2)

As a modeler, I want the fix for reversed faces to avoid breaking the rendering of faces that already display correctly so existing extrusion workflows remain stable.

**Why this priority**: The bug fix should restore missing surfaces without introducing new visual regressions in already-correct models.

**Independent Test**: Open representative existing extrusion scenes that already render correctly and confirm they still appear complete after the change.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an extrusion whose faces already render correctly, **When** the user views the body after this change, **Then** the visible exterior surfaces still appear complete.
2. **Given** a closed solid contains hidden faces behind nearer geometry, **When** the body is rendered, **Then** those hidden faces remain occluded instead of showing through.

---

### Edge Cases

- When a single extruded body contains both correctly oriented and reversed faces, all externally visible faces should still appear without gaps.
- When the camera views a face from the inside or back side, the face should remain visible if it is not occluded by nearer geometry.
- When multiple faces overlap in depth, rendering both sides must not cause farther faces to show through nearer surfaces.
- When an extrusion produces very thin geometry, both exposed sides should remain visible without making hidden interior surfaces bleed through closed regions.

## Verification & Evidence *(mandatory)*

### Required Evidence

- **Automated Coverage**: No dedicated automated viewport regression is assumed for this rendering defect; if practical, capture any focused regression coverage that can prove reversed-orientation extrude faces stay visible.
- **Manual Validation**: 1) Open elcad. 2) Create or load a sketch that reproduces the missing-face extrusion bug. 3) Extrude the profile. 4) Confirm the previously missing face is visible immediately after extrusion. 5) Orbit to the opposite side of that face and confirm it remains visible. 6) Repeat with several representative extrusions, including one that already rendered correctly before the fix. 7) Confirm hidden faces do not show through nearer geometry.
- **Artifacts / Measurements**: Capture before/after screenshots or video showing the same extruded face from front and back viewpoints, plus any build output or diagnostic evidence used to confirm the regression is resolved.

## Requirements *(mandatory)*

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: The system MUST render every externally visible face produced by an extrusion regardless of whether the face orientation points toward or away from the viewer.
- **FR-002**: The system MUST keep extrude-generated faces visible when the user views them from either side during normal viewport navigation.
- **FR-003**: The system MUST apply this behavior consistently to all face types produced by extrusion, including caps, side walls, and any subdivided faces created by the resulting shape.
- **FR-004**: The system MUST preserve normal depth-based occlusion so that hidden faces remain hidden behind nearer geometry.
- **FR-005**: The system MUST preserve existing rendering behavior for extruded faces that already displayed correctly before this fix.
- **FR-006**: The system MUST allow downstream viewport interactions that rely on the rendered face, such as visual inspection and face-targeted selection, to proceed without the face disappearing because of orientation alone.

### Key Entities *(include if feature involves data)*

- **Extruded Face**: A surface created as part of turning a sketch profile into a 3D body.
- **Face Orientation**: The facing direction associated with a rendered surface, which may be correct or reversed relative to the viewer.
- **Visible Surface Region**: Any part of an extruded face that should appear in the viewport because it is exposed and not blocked by nearer geometry.

## Success Criteria *(mandatory)*

### Measurable Outcomes

- **SC-001**: In manual validation across at least five representative extrusions, 100% of externally visible extrude-generated faces remain visible from both front-side and back-side viewpoints.
- **SC-002**: Reviewers can orbit around each validation model through a full inspection pass without observing any outward-facing extruded face disappear because of its orientation.
- **SC-003**: In the known reproduction case for this bug, the previously missing face is visible immediately after extrusion and remains visible after the camera is moved to the opposite side.
- **SC-004**: Existing comparison scenes that already rendered correctly show no new see-through artifacts or missing exterior faces during manual review.

## Assumptions

- The underlying extrusion already creates the correct geometry, and this feature only addresses viewport visibility of generated faces.
- The requested outcome is to make faces visible from both sides; changing surface shading characteristics beyond what is necessary for visibility is out of scope.
- This feature applies to faces generated by extrusion workflows and does not redefine how unrelated non-extruded geometry is rendered.
- Standard hidden-surface behavior should remain intact so that rendering both sides does not expose faces that are fully blocked by nearer geometry.
